Petition For Release And Claim Of Funds After Interpleader
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gregory Martin appeared for and with Gayle Martin (by telephone).

The Court stated that in June the Court directed the Parties to set up a trust, that the trust was set up by Ms. Martin, but Ms. Martin didn't own the property, that the trust should be set up by the owner of the property, that Asia is the owner and is a minor, and questions whether she needs a Guardian Ad Litem. Mr. Martin stated that Asia had a Guardian Ad Litem, that Stephen Conley was appointed in 2013 to sign the release, but not ongoing, provided history, stated that there was a settlement, that Gayle was to retain 70% of her life tenancy, that Gayle was to be reimbursed for maintenance and expenses of the property in the amount $60,000.00, and that Leandrus got 10%, Armando got 10% and Asia was to get 10%. Mr. Martin also stated that there was litigation on Armando's petition, that they mediated, that $10,000.00 of Gayle's money was held, and that in August of 2017 there was an agreement between Armando and Gayle so that the property could be sold and for reimbursement of Leandrus' 10% and Gayle's $10,000.00. The Court suggested that Asia's share be deposited into a blocked account, stated that it is troubling that Asia has no Guardian Ad Litem and that someone needs to set up a blocked until she turns 18 years of age. Ms. Martin stated that Asia will turn 18 on September 16, 2020 and that the settlement releases her funds at age 25. The Petition for Release and Claim of Funds After Interpleader is continued to February 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Martin is to provide a supplement and prove up the $60,000.00 in expenses/address the costs and provide status of the blocked account. The supplement is to be filed by February 11, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Martin for and with Gayle Benton Martin (petitioner).

The record shall reflect that the Court contacted Mr. Michael Kelly who represents the respondent Armando Lee Benton and Mr. Kelly has no contest to the tentative ruling and does not wish to appear via telephonic appearance.

The Court informed Mr. Martin that he did not follow proper procedure and explained the tentative ruling rule. Mr. Martin stated that he did make attempts to check the tentative ruling last night. Furthermore Mr. Martin presented oral argument in support of his position to his motion to consolidate. The Court stated that the requests that are being made pertain to civil jury trail issues and that the probate department can not accommodate jury trials.

After hearing oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner's Motion to Consolidate these actions in the Probate Court is denied, without prejudice as to filing a motion to consolidate in the related civil case. The parties are encouraged to engage in mediation. The Court shall prepare a form of order.
